report_name: LockBit 5.0 Ransomware Gang Claims Attack on Singapore's SCB Group

threat_actors.name: LockBit 5.0
threat_actors.description: The LockBit 5.0 ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation claimed responsibility for a cyberattack against SCB Group. The group and its affiliates employ a double-extortion model, exfiltrating data before encryption and threatening public release to coerce payment.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion of victim organizations through ransomware and data leakage threats.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors allegedly exfiltrated sensitive data from the victim's network before encryption as part of their double-extortion tactic.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The LockBit 5.0 ransomware was used to encrypt files across SCB Group's network to disrupt operations and demand a ransom.

attack_patterns.name: External Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: LockBit affiliates often gain initial access by exploiting vulnerabilities in public-facing infrastructure like VPNs or RDP servers.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping: LSASS Memory
attack_patterns.description: LockBit affiliates use tools like Mimikatz to harvest credentials from memory to escalate privileges and move laterally.

attack_patterns.name: SMB/Windows Admin Shares
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use stolen credentials to spread across the network via SMB/Windows Admin Shares, often using tools like PsExec.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: LockBit is known to delete Volume Shadow Copies to prevent system recovery from local backups.

attack_patterns.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
attack_patterns.description: LockBit affiliates use WMI to execute their payload on remote systems for lateral movement.

malware.name: LockBit
malware.description: The LockBit ransomware payload, specifically the '5.0' version, was used to encrypt files on the SCB Group network. It appends the '.lockbit' extension to encrypted files.

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.description: Used by LockBit affiliates to harvest credentials from memory for privilege escalation and lateral movement.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: A legitimate Sysinternals tool used by attackers to execute payloads on remote systems for lateral movement.

tools.name: wmic
tools.description: The Windows Management Instrumentation command-line utility is used by LockBit affiliates to remotely execute commands and malware as part of lateral movement.

tools.name: procdump
tools.description: A legitimate Sysinternals tool that can be used by attackers to dump process memory, such as LSASS, to extract credentials.

identities.name: SCB Group
identities.description: A construction company based in Singapore targeted by the LockBit 5.0 ransomware group in a double-extortion attack.

infrastructure.name: LockBit Dark Web Leak Site
infrastructure.description: The dark web site used by the LockBit RaaS operation to publicly name their victims and threaten the release of stolen data to extort ransom payments.

indicators.name: LockBit Affiliate Tooling - PsExec and ProcDump
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: LockBit Affiliate Tooling Execution
id: 2b1a9c8d-4e5f-4a3b-9c8d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PsExec.exe or procdump.exe, which are commonly used by LockBit affiliates for lateral movement and credential dumping.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/lockbit-5-0-targets-scb-group-in-singapore-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1021.002
  - attack.t1003.001
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\PsExec.exe'
      - '\procdump.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of legitimate Sysinternals tools (PsExec.exe, procdump.exe) often used by LockBit affiliates.

indicators.name: LockBit Registry Key
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: LockBit Ransomware Registry Key
id: 8c7b6a5d-4e3f-4a2b-9c8d-1e2f3a4b5c6e
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a registry key commonly used by LockBit to store configuration or mark infection.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/lockbit-5-0-targets-scb-group-in-singapore-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: high
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetObject|endswith: '\Software\LockBit'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a registry key created by LockBit to store configuration or mark the system as infected.

indicators.name: LockBit WMI Lateral Movement
indicators.pattern:
title: LockBit WMI Lateral Movement
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of wmic.exe to create a remote process, a technique used by LockBit for lateral movement.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/lockbit-5-0-targets-scb-group-in-singapore-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1047
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'process call create'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of WMI for remote command execution, a common lateral movement technique for LockBit.

indicators.name: LockBit Encrypted File Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: LockBit Encrypted File Detected
id: 9d8e7f6a-5b4c-4a3b-9c8d-1e2f3a4b5c6f
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files with the .lockbit extension, indicating LockBit ransomware activity.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/lockbit-5-0-targets-scb-group-in-singapore-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.lockbit'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects files being renamed with the '.lockbit' extension, a definitive sign of LockBit encryption.

indicators.name: LockBit Recovery Inhibition via bcdedit
indicators.pattern:
title: LockBit Recovery Inhibition via bcdedit
id: 7a6b5c4d-3e2f-4a1b-9c8d-1e2f3a4b5c7g
status: experimental
description: 'Detects the use of bcdedit to ignore all boot failures, a technique used by some ransomware variants like LockBit to inhibit system recovery.'
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/lockbit-5-0-targets-scb-group-in-singapore-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1490
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\bcdedit.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '/set'
      - 'bootstatuspolicy'
      - 'ignoreallfailures'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a command-line pattern using bcdedit.exe to inhibit system recovery, a known LockBit TTP.
